rationale: The citing paper uses Berger and Milkman (2012) in its hypothesis development, not just as a background list citation. It cites that work as evidence that people tend to share positive news/events and that positive sharing can improve recipients' mood, which supports the citing paper's self-enhancement account of why low interpersonal closeness leads to more positive WOM. The citation is also revisited in discussion/limitations when considering relational consequences of negative sharing. Although no methods or data are reused, the cited paper materially informs the conceptual framing.
relation_description: Dubois et al. draw on Berger and Milkman (2012) as prior conceptual evidence about valence in social transmission, especially the social advantages of sharing positive content. They use this evidence to motivate and interpret their own theory that interpersonal closeness shifts WOM valence via self-enhancement versus protection motives.
